name: Two-Zone Spine Corridor Inn -- Single Wing (Offset Tier)

zone_placement_rules.notes: inn.pattern.md: the door centers on the common room's own sub-axis, not the midpoint of the full building footprint.
inn.pattern.md: 'likely near the common room, given that is the only zone with open approach floor.'

variants.notes: One guest_quarters_wing cluster off the spine corridor. WO-0059 split: this tier now carries only the single-wing variant; the directly-observed mirrored-dual-wing configuration moved to LAY-INTERIOR-INN-ZONED-EXPANDED so each of the three tiers is a distinct, unambiguous LayoutFamily artifact.

provenance.notes: This single-wing configuration is an evidence-respecting reduction from the one directly observed sample (Map020.json), which actually shows mirrored upper/lower wings -- see LAY-INTERIOR-INN-ZONED-EXPANDED for that literal configuration. Removing one wing is a straightforward, low-risk simplification of an observed layout, not an unevidenced invention, so confidence stays medium (matching the source pattern's own ceiling) rather than dropping to experimental.
